ai.issueType: Missing lang attribute on html element
ai.description: The html element lacks a lang attribute, which prevents screen readers from correctly announcing the page language and pronunciation rules to users.
The link has an empty aria-label attribute that overrides the visible text content, resulting in no accessible name for screen reader users.
The link text "Click here" does not describe the destination or purpose of the link, forcing screen reader users to rely on surrounding context to understand where the link leads.
The logo image lacks an alt attribute, preventing screen readers from conveying the purpose or content of the image to blind users.

ai.issueType: Element with role img missing accessible name
ai.description: The div element has role="img" but lacks an aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title attribute to provide an accessible name for screen readers.
The email input has no associated label element or aria-label, so screen readers cannot identify the field's purpose to users.
The textarea has no associated label, preventing screen readers from identifying that the field is for entering a message.
The fieldset element lacks a legend child to describe the group of form controls, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand the purpose of this form section.

ai.issueType: Table data cell used as header without th semantics
ai.description: The cell contains "Plan" which is a column header, but uses td instead of th, failing to convey the tabular structure and header relationships to assistive technologies.
The data table lacks a caption element to provide a visible title or description, making it harder for screen reader users to understand the table's purpose and content.
The heading hierarchy jumps from h2 to h4, skipping h3, which disrupts the logical navigation structure for screen reader users who rely on heading levels to understand page organization.
Multiple section elements exist on the page, but this section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from other section landmarks in screen reader landmark lists.
The button with text "Menu" controls expandable content but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the state.
The email input collects personal information but lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with filling in their known email address.

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
lighthouse.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

lighthouse.issueType: `<html>` element does not have a `[lang]` attribute
lighthouse.description: If a page doesn't specify a `lang` attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. [Learn more about the `lang` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/html-has-lang).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Document does not have a main landmark.
lighthouse.description: One main landmark helps screen reader users navigate a web page. [Learn more about landmarks](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/landmark-one-main).

lighthouse.issueType: `<td>` elements in a large `<table>` do not have table headers.
lighthouse.description: Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring that `<td>` elements in a large table (3 or more cells in width and height) have an associated table header may improve the experience for screen reader users. [Learn more about table headers](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/td-has-header).
